- Click at (0,42) only gave 1 conversion (42→41), so boundary hypothesis was wrong.
- 2-conversion clicks so far: cols 47, 50, 36, 56, 44 (across different rows). No clear pattern.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First, let's check the current board state
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
pink = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'P']
charcoal = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'c']
print(f"Pink pixels: {pink[0]['pixels'] if pink else 0}")
print(f"Charcoal nodes: {len(charcoal)}")

# Check the segmentation to understand the layout
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['P', 'c', 'B', 'w', 'g', 'G']:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}...")

# Test clicking at col 36 on row 0 (gave 2 conversions at row 25)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 0, 'col': 36}])
pink_after = [n for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'P']
print(f"After (0,36): pink={pink_after[0]['pixels'] if pink_after else 0}, diff={41 - (pink_after[0]['pixels'] if pink_after else 0)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
